intro: How connected is the house, really? We independently checked the listing's claims against transit and routing sources. The short answer: better than the brochure suggests — a direct hourly train reaches central Munich in well under an hour, and the airport is a straightforward drive.

facts.label: To central Munich by train
facts.detail: A direct RB66 regional train (Kochelseebahn, DB Regio) runs Bernried → München Hbf hourly — no transfer needed. The exposé framed this as a ~60-min bus+rail trip; it is in fact a single ~39-min ride.

facts.label: Train frequency
facts.detail: The Kochelseebahn (Tutzing–Penzberg–Kochel) runs roughly hourly, densified to half-hourly on the Tutzing–Penzberg section at peak times.

facts.label: Walk / bike to Bernried station
facts.detail: Bernried(Starnberger See) station is ~0.9 km from the house, reachable on foot or by bike — no car needed to catch the train.

facts.label: To Munich Airport (MUC)
facts.detail: Free-flow driving is ~58–65 min via A95 → A99 → A92 (the brochure's '99 min' is a heavy-traffic worst case). By public transit it's ~1 h 40 with 1–2 transfers, since the airport sits on the far NE side of Munich.

facts.label: Nearest Autobahn (A95)
facts.detail: The A95 (Munich–Garmisch) is reached at the Seeshaupt junction (AS 7), putting both Munich and the Alps on a fast road.

facts.label: Neighboring towns by train
facts.detail: Direct RB66 stops. Tutzing is the everyday hub (big supermarket, schools, sailing); Seeshaupt is the nearest pharmacy and pediatrician. Starnberg and Weilheim need a change at Tutzing.

facts.label: To the Alps by car
facts.detail: Down the A95: the Kochelsee/Walchensee lake-and-ski area in ~30–45 min, Garmisch-Partenkirchen in ~50–60 min. Weekend mountains are genuinely close.

facts.label: Regional bus (MVV 948)
facts.detail: Line 948 (Penzberg–Seeshaupt–Bernried–Tutzing) serves the village but is school-oriented, Mon–Fri, no weekends. The train is the useful link.
